revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:福島縣護國神社]] {{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017, 09:05 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Fukushima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image = |location = Komayama 1, [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} |deity = National martyrs
[[Amaterasu]] |Rank = Minister of Home Affairs Designated Gokoku Shrine・Special Shrine |founded = {{ill|1879|en|1879|qid=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}} (Meiji 12) |festival = {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|qid=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}}・{{ill|September 23|en|September 23|qid=Q2888|lt=September 23|1=September 23}} |latDeg = 37|LatMin = 46|LatSec = 03.8 |longDeg = 140|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 05.4 |map = Japan Fukushima|Label Position = left }} '''Fukushima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (福島縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on [[Mount Shinobu]] in [[:en:Fukushima City|Fukushima City]], {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It enshrines over 68,500 souls who died in wars from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. == History == The shrine was established on {{ill|October 4|en|October 4|qid=Q2929|lt=October 4|1=October 4}}, {{ill|1879|en|1879|qid=Q7783|lt=1879|1=1879}} (Meiji 12) by consolidating the souls of Boshin War soldiers from the three locations of Soma, Miharu, and Wakamatsu, as well as those who had died in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}. It was originally founded as the Shobu-yama Shōkonsha under government patronage. In {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Shōwa 12), the current shrine buildings were constructed, and in {{ill|1939|en|1939|qid=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}} (Shōwa 14), it was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the [[Minister of Home Affairs (Japan)|Minister of Home Affairs]], thus being renamed Fukushima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. During the occupation by the [[General Headquarters, Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers|GHQ]] after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was renamed Dairei Shrine and enshrined Amaterasu Ōmikami to ensure its survival. In {{ill|September|en|September|qid=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} {{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=1952|1=1952}} (Shōwa 27), following the restoration of sovereignty, it reverted to its original name, Fukushima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. == Sub-Shrines == * Shinobu-yama Tenmangū ** Enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. It was founded in {{ill|1988|en|1988|qid=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} (Shōwa 63) by inviting a divided spirit from {{ill|Dazaifu Tenmangū|en|Dazaifu Tenmangū|qid=Q710786|lt=Dazaifu Tenmangū|1=Dazaifu Tenmangū}}. == External Links == * [http://www.gokoku559.info/ Official website of Fukushima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[File:Fukushimaken Gokoku shrine1.JPG|270px]] [[File:Fukushimaken Gokoku Shrine2.JPG|thumb|right|Shrine grounds]] {{translated page|ja|福島縣護國神社|version=101990335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:福島縣護國神社]] {{Commons category|Fukushimaken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11593728}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukushima City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukushima City]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Fukushima, Hokkaidō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
